r-Encoding: 7bit Content-Type: text/plain; charset=us-ascii Content-Length: 721 Status: OR The structure w/ a spherical 5-bar differential was tested. W/o the wing: First resonance found at ~270 Hz and next at ~450Hz. At 1st resonance, flapping g

and next at ~450Hz. At 1st resonance, flapping gain was ~5 deg/V (input 0-6V => output 30 deg motion). Q=~14 Adding a 1.5mg, 15mm long wing: First resonance found at ~68 Hz and next at ~310 Hz. At 1st resonance, flapping gain was ~0.83 deg/V (in

t 1st resonance, flapping gain was ~0.83 deg/V (input 0-60V => output 50 deg motion). Q=~2.3 The amplitude for rotation was pretty bad (no measurement... just observation) so I was thinking that this could be one of the 1-DOF backups for show. I'm

could be one of the 1-DOF backups for show. I'm thinking about cutting off some of the distal part of the wing in order to lower J and b to get the resonance frequency back up. Any suggestions? Thanks, Joe
